title: Humor is the way to girls — choose the hard way
html:
TL;DR

  • Two-line November 2019 aphorism: title "Humor is the way to girls," body "Choose the hard way."
  • The body's "choose the hard way" is the hinge — Yahir is reframing humor as the high-investment strategy (vs. the easy path of looks/status), not the easy one.
  • Sits in the how you react / expensive doesnt equal margin aphorism cluster — three same-week one-liners stacking long-game-over-short-game logic.
  • Earliest extant humor-as-attraction-tool note in the corpus; rhymes forward to the much-later Emily arc dynamic where Yahir's self-reported edge is "banter" and tone control.
  • Foreshadows the choose the hard way motif that recurs in his manifesto entries across multiple years.

Notes

On the surface the title reads as standard high-school-boy folk wisdom — be funny, get girls. But the body line "choose the hard way" completely reframes the proposition: humor is now coded as the costly signal, the strategy that takes more reps, more risk, and more taste than the easy paths (looks, money, status, social-proof). The teen is doing the 10x thinking move on a romance prescription.

This is consistent with the broader November-2019 mood: Yahir is on a the-real-move-is-the-harder-move kick. 2019 11 03 expensive doesnt equal margin separates price from margin. 2019 11 09 it doesnt matter react (the 11-09 DaBaby aphorism) prioritizes reaction over event. This 11-10 note slots into the same logic: don't take the cheap path even if it's culturally legible.

The humor-as-investment frame holds across years — by 2026 the Emily arc writeups consistently identify Yahir's relational edge as conversational tone and timing, not appearance or status. The teen aphorism survived as a working theory of self.

Privacy note

None.

Connections

  • 2019 11 09 it doesnt matter react · 2019 11 03 expensive doesnt equal margin — November aphorism cluster
  • 2019 09 10 low key writing this to end — relational-strategy ancestor
  • the emily arc — long-arc descendant (humor-as-edge)
  • choose the hard way — concept stub from this note
  • · 10x thinking · grants pass teen era
  • no runway discipline — adjacent harder-path-as-edge sibling
